sections.name: Pater secreto
lines: /:«Our Father» is said silently until «And lead us not into temptation:».:/

sections.name: Pater totum secreto
lines: /:«Our Father» is said completely in silence.:/

sections.name: Credo secreto
lines: /:«Credo» is said silently until «The resurrection of the body.»:/

sections.name: Pater post_
lines: /:Thereafter, only « Pater Noster » is said silently, unless another Hour follows immediately.:/

sections.name: Pater post V_
lines: /:And if thus the Office is ended, only « Pater Noster » is said silently.:/

sections.name: Ant Maria post
lines: /:If the Choir is to be left: after its recitation, follows « Dóminus det. » and immediately, one of the Final Antiphones of Our Lady with its Versicle and Oration. (cf. Laudes):/

sections.name: Secreto_
lines: /:silently:/

sections.name: Secreto_C
lines: /:In choir, after the Little Office of Our Lady has been finished, is said silently::/

sections.name: Secreto a Laudibus
lines: /:If Matins and Lauds are separated, then is said silently::/
(sed rubrica monastica)
/:On Simplex feasts and on ferial days, and if Matins are separate from Lauds, one begins in silence::/

sections.name: Clara voce_
lines: /:Thereafter, with hearable voice, the Versicle is said::/

sections.name: Secus absolute
lines: /:Otherwise, the hour is started directly as follows::/
(sed rubrica ^Trident)
/:After the hymn «Te Deum laudamus» or the last responsory has been recited, the hour begins directly as follows::/

sections.name: Matutinum
lines: !Skip the rest, unless praying %Lauds% separately.
(sed rubrica tridentina)

!After the hymn Te Deum laudamus or the ninth or third responsory has been recited, one begins directly with the %Laudes%, with the exception of Christmas Night: in this one prays the Oratio and then the Christmas mass is celebrated as it is stated at the appropriate place.

sections.name: Matutinum
lines: !Skip the rest, unless praying %Lauds% separately.
(sed rubrica tridentina aut rubrica divino)
!Skip the rest in Offices of 12 lessons, unless praying %Lauds% separately. On Simplex feasts and on ferial days (be it with three or one reading) the following is always prayed.

sections.name: Deinde secreto
lines: /:Thereafter is said silently:/

sections.name: PAC ad Completorium
lines: /:If right after Compline, Matins is said, then «Our Father, Hail Mary» and «Credo,» are said twice, once each for end of the Office of the previous day, and for the beginning of the Office of the next.:/

sections.name: DefunctV
lines: &Dominus_vobiscum
&Benedicamus_Domino
! After Vespers of the day, %Vespers of the Dead% are said.

sections.name: DefunctM
lines: &Dominus_vobiscum
&Benedicamus_Domino
! After Lauds of the day, %Matins and Lauds of the Dead% are said.

sections.name: Martyrologium
lines: /:Then the Martyrology is read in Choir.:/
(sed rubrica cisterciensis)

/:Here Prime ends. Thereafter, the Martyrology is read in the Chapter Hall without blessing. Outside the convent, the Martyrology is not read but one immediately continues with « Pretiósa. » as below.:/

sections.name: Regula
lines: /:Even outside of Choir, it is laudible to read the Holy Regula; otherwise, the Short Reading is prayed:/
(sed rubrica cisterciensis)
/:In private recitation, here follows the Short Reading all year round.:/

sections.name: Regula ante
lines: /:In the Chapter Hall, a chapter or parts of a chapter from the Rule of S. Benedict is read.:/

sections.name: examen
lines: /:There follows an examination of conscience, or the Our Father said silently.:/

sections.name: Preces flexis genibus
lines: /:kneel:/

sections.name: Antiphona
lines: /:intoned solely when the office is chanted:/

sections.name: NonaAdjuncta
lines: /:The ninth lesson of the office is either omitted or may be joined to the eight.:/
